commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=72d190b8358adf0b084097b1e5fc26191239fb96)
commands.review_summary:
The PR changes `resolveEffectiveModelFallbacks` so user-sourced model overrides return configured agent/default fallback lists instead of disabling model fallback.

PR surface: Source +10. Total +10 across 1 file.

Reproducibility: yes. source inspection reproduces the reported no-fallback path: current `resolveEffectiveModelFallbacks` returns `[]` for `modelOverrideSource: "user"`, and `runWithModelFallback` then has no fallback candidates. I did not run a live provider quota exhaustion scenario in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Maintainers need to decide the `/model` pin contract and require contributor proof before any automated repair or merge path is safe.

target.title: fix: user-pinned model falls back to global chain on quota exhaustion
target.body:
## Problem

When a user selects a model via `/model <name>`, `modelOverride` is stored in `sessions.json` with `modelOverrideSource: "user"`. The `resolveEffectiveModelFallbacks` function returns `[]` for user-sourced overrides, making `hasFallbackCandidates = false`.

When the pinned model hits a **long-term** failure (weekly/monthly quota exhaustion, subscription limit), every subsequent message fails with:

```
⚠️ All models are temporarily rate-limited. Please try again in a few minutes.
```

This is a complete outage — no recovery path short of manually editing `sessions.json`.

**Root cause** (`src/agents/agent-scope.ts`):

```typescript
if (!canUseConfiguredFallbacks) {
  return [];  // disables entire fallback chain for user-pinned sessions
}
```

**Reproduction:**
1. Use `/model glm-5` (or any model with a weekly quota)
2. Exhaust the weekly quota (error 1310 from zhipuai)
3. Every new message fails — even after gateway restart — because `sessions.json` persists `modelOverride` across sessions and new sessions inherit the pin

## Fix

Return the global fallback chain instead of `[]`. The user's intent when pinning a model is to **prefer** it, not to disable recovery when it's unavailable. The pinned model remains the **first** candidate and is always tried first.

## Behavior change

| Scenario | Before | After |
|---|---|---|
| User-pinned model temporarily rate-limited | Error surfaced | Falls back to next model |
| User-pinned model quota exhausted (days/weeks) | Permanent outage | Falls back to next model |
| No model pin / auto-fallback pin | Unchanged | Unchanged |
target.author: knowcheng
